title: Impossible to use `v4/cmd/podman/registry` Golang package on OS X
body:
I am using the latest Podman for OS X:

```shell
#podman -v
podman version 4.2.0
```

A simple program that tries to get Podman info similar to the [`podman-info`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/system/info.go):

```go
package main

import (
	"github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry"
)

func main() {
	registry.IsRemote()
}
```

fails to build:

```
package podmantest
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod
	imports github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/cni: build constraints exclude all Go files in <my system path>/go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/common@v0.49.1/libnetwork/cni
package podmantest
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra
	imports github.com/containers/podman/v4/libpod
	imports github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/network: build constraints exclude all Go files in <my system path>/go/pkg/mod/github.com/containers/common@v0.49.1/libnetwork/network
```

To recreate program dependencies:

```shell
go get github.com/containers/podman/v4 
go mod tidy
```

From a brief inspection it seems that there are no Darwin implementation for [github.com/containers/common/libnetwork/network](https://github.com/containers/common/tree/main/libnetwork/network).

I am wondering, how the OS X version of Podman CLI is even built?

comments.body: Podman on Macs and Windows are Podman *clients* that interact with a remote Podman.  For example, the podman client on the mac can do a `podman machine init --now` and then interact with podman on the virtual machine.  Most internal podman code only can be compiled and run on Linux.

I am aware about such limitation. However, `github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry` is a part of the API client. And from my perspective it should work on any machine where Podman can be installed.

> I am wondering, how the OS X version of Podman CLI is even built?

Compiling "podman-remote" requires a special build flag, `remote`

```go
//go:build remote
// +build remote

package main
```
@afbjorklund, is there any way to make `github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry` Go library work on OS X?

I still believe this is a defect. This package returns Podman system information and is essential to writing cross-platform tools that use Podman as a runtime for local services. At the moment I have to parse whatever `podman info` returns to me.

Podman is a Linux tool, it is not cross-platform. You can access it remotely (from all platforms), but that's a different story...

For this particular piece of the code, there is a "mode" that tells if it is running locally (ABIMode) or remotely (TunnelMode).

There are also a bunch of other secret flags, you can find them by doing `make podman-remote` or similar.

`go build -tags "remote exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper containers_image_openpgp"`

```Makefile
REMOTETAGS ?= remote exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper containers_image_openpgp
```

But the best is probably to use the [remote client API](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html) (or even docker), then you only need to bother about REST.

* https://podman.io/blogs/2020/08/10/podman-go-bindings.html

* https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/pkg/bindings

Yes, using the bindings is the recommended way to interact with Podman programmatically in Go.  `pkg/bindings` was designed to be easy to use and work cross-platform.  Other Go packages in the stack were not necessarily designed in a way to be used outside of podman.
The main reason to consider "docker" (compat) was to get a stable API, but I think this is being considered for Podman v5 ?

> The main reason to consider "docker" (compat) was to get a stable API, but I think this is being considered for Podman v5 ?

What do you mean @afbjorklund ?

I meant that if you target Podman v4 API today, it should remain compatible also with Podman v5 ?
(that was not always the case)
